title: OpenAPI 3.2, Compatibility, Deployment, and Production Defense · Runtime Contract: Contract Runtime Drift
summary: An undocumented API changes nullable fields, serves stale generated docs, deploys a new binary before migrations, drains no traffic, and cannot tie an incident response to source or schema. Planned artifact: Add and verify the httpsgo contract runtime drift increment in versioned API contract and production release defense. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Compare specification, route registration, transport types, validators, examples, tests, auth policy, and observed responses to detect bidirectional drift.
Describe OpenAPI 3.2 operations, servers, parameters, request bodies, responses, problems, schemas, security, callbacks, and webhooks with tool limits.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Go server Predict Contract Runtime Drift · Theory Contract Runtime Drift
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory team handles case 4178 in a privacy-sensitive records API using conditional requests. Build deterministic Go evidence to gate one OpenAPI-compatible, expand-first, canary, drain, and rollback release; then replay the same operation key with different content and reject the mismatch. Browser code models decisions only. Listener, TLS, database, proxy, race, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Contract Runtime Drift contract for the supplied data.

Evidence target: Go server Predict Contract Runtime Drift · Theory Contract Runtime Drift.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for compare specification, route registration, transport types, validators, examples, tests, auth policy, and observed responses to detect bidirectional drift. For Go server Predict Contract Runtime Drift · Theory Contract Runtime Drift, keep Contract Runtime Drift connected to executable HTTP client decision evidence.
